perceptual_narrative_logs.visual_narrative_log: (1) A young woman with dark hair, wearing a white shirt and a black vest, is seen in the book section of a Costco warehouse. (2) She begins flipping through a large, colorful pop-up book titled 'Sea Animal Sounds.' (3) As she turns the pages, various 3D paper constructions of sea creatures like whales, coral reefs, sharks, and albatrosses emerge. (4) The woman points at the text in the book and later holds the open book up to the camera while smiling and looking expressive. (5) In the final segment, the back of the book is shown, revealing a battery compartment and an on/off switch, followed by a close-up of the book cover.
perceptual_narrative_logs.audio_narrative_log: (1) The video begins with the woman's voice excitedly announcing they are at Costco and have found something amazing. (2) As the pages of the book are turned, high-quality electronic sound effects of ocean waves and whale vocalizations are clearly audible. (3) A male voice off-screen joins the conversation, discussing the book's features and noting that it's in English. (4) The woman imitates a whale's low-pitched, haunting call, which is followed by loud laughter from both speakers. (5) The male narrator provides a final summary of the product's price and its battery-saving features.
perceptual_narrative_logs.text_narrative_log: (1) 'Wait a second, we are at Costco' - Functional, setting the scene. (2) 'Found a magical thing' - Hook, creating curiosity. (3) 'Worth buying - kids love talking storybooks' - Promotional/Recommendation. (4) 'Listen carefully' - Directive to the audience. (5) 'This is a book with sound' - Functional explanation. (6) 'No Chinese translation' - Informative critique. (7) 'Hahahaha' - Emotional/Atmospheric. (8) 'Sea Animal Sounds Pop-up Sound Book' - Functional product title. (9) 'Price 599' - Informative.

temporal_pacing_summary.mainActionFlow: Intro -> Product Demonstration -> Humorous Interaction -> Product Specification/Review

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: 1 main female creator on screen; 1 male narrator visible only by his hands holding the book.
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: A young Asian woman with long dark hair, wearing a white blouse under a black sleeveless vest.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: Stacked shelves of books, puzzles, and toys in a large warehouse-style store with high ceilings and industrial lighting.

audio_production_style.audioTransition: Natural field transitions with hard cuts for scene changes.

narrative_and_purpose.narrativeStructure: List-based: Hook -> Physical Demo -> Sensory Experience -> Evaluation
narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: The video serves as a shopping recommendation and entertaining product review. By demonstrating the unique 'Sea Animal Sounds' pop-up book at Costco, the creators aim to share a 'good find' with their audience, emphasizing its interactive features (sounds and 3D art) while providing entertainment through the creator's playful imitation of animal noises.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: Frequent display of the store logo, specific pricing information, and a strong verbal recommendation of the product's value.
endorsementObject: Garry Fleming's Sea Animal Sounds Pop-Up Book

social_sociology_tags.uniqueSignatures: On-screen variety-show style captions and playful banter between the camera person and the host.
social_sociology_tags.freeDescribe: The video effectively uses the 'treasure hunt' appeal of Costco to engage viewers. The contrast between the English-only content of the book and the Mandarin commentary makes it relatable to local parents.
